capabilities.description: Returns one or more capabilities associated with this partnership.

nextToken.description: When additional results are obtained from the command, a NextToken parameter is returned in the output. You can then pass the NextToken parameter in a subsequent command to continue listing additional resources.

capabilityId.description: Returns a system-assigned unique identifier for the capability.

type.description: Returns the type of the capability. Currently, only edi is supported.

createdAt.description: Returns a timestamp for creation date and time of the capability.

modifiedAt.description: Returns a timestamp that identifies the most recent date and time that the capability was modified.

nextToken.description: When additional results are obtained from the command, a NextToken parameter is returned in the output. You can then pass the NextToken parameter in a subsequent command to continue listing additional resources.

profileId.description: Returns the unique, system-generated identifier for the profile connected to this partnership.

partnershipId.description: Returns the unique, system-generated identifier for a partnership.

capabilities.description: Returns one or more capabilities associated with this partnership.

capabilityOptions.description: Contains the details for an Outbound EDI capability.

outboundEdi.description: A structure that contains the outbound EDI options.

x12.description: A structure that contains an X12 envelope structure.

common.description: A container for the X12 outbound EDI headers.

wrapOptions.description: Contains options for wrapping (line folding) in X12 EDI files. Wrapping controls how long lines are handled in the EDI output.

inboundEdi.description: A structure that contains the inbound EDI options for the capability.

x12.description: A structure that contains X12-specific options for processing inbound X12 EDI files.

acknowledgmentOptions.description: Specifies acknowledgment options for inbound X12 EDI files. These options control how functional and technical acknowledgments are handled.

tradingPartnerId.description: Returns the unique, system-generated identifier for a trading partner.

createdAt.description: Returns a timestamp for creation date and time of the partnership.

modifiedAt.description: Returns a timestamp that identifies the most recent date and time that the partnership was modified.

nextToken.description: When additional results are obtained from the command, a NextToken parameter is returned in the output. You can then pass the NextToken parameter in a subsequent command to continue listing additional resources.

profileId.description: Returns the unique, system-generated identifier for the profile.

businessName.description: Returns the name for the business associated with this profile.

logging.description: Specifies whether or not logging is enabled for this profile.

logGroupName.description: Returns the name of the logging group.

createdAt.description: Returns the timestamp for creation date and time of the profile.

modifiedAt.description: Returns the timestamp that identifies the most recent date and time that the profile was modified.

transformers.description:
Returns an array of one or more transformer objects.

For each transformer, a TransformerSummary object is returned. The TransformerSummary contains all the details for a specific transformer.

nextToken.description: When additional results are obtained from the command, a NextToken parameter is returned in the output. You can then pass the NextToken parameter in a subsequent command to continue listing additional resources.

transformerId.description: Returns the system-assigned unique identifier for the transformer.

name.description: Returns the descriptive name for the transformer.

status.description: Returns the state of the newly created transformer. The transformer can be either active or inactive. For the transformer to be used in a capability, its status must active.

createdAt.description: Returns a timestamp indicating when the transformer was created. For example, 2023-07-20T19:58:44.624Z.

modifiedAt.description: Returns a timestamp representing the date and time for the most recent change for the transformer object.

fileFormat.description: Returns that the currently supported file formats for EDI transformations are JSON and XML.

mappingTemplate.description: Returns the mapping template for the transformer. This template is used to map the parsed EDI file using JSONata or XSLT.

ediType.description: Returns the details for the EDI standard that is being used for the transformer. Currently, only X12 is supported. X12 is a set of standards and corresponding messages that define specific business documents.

x12Details.description: Returns the details for the EDI standard that is being used for the transformer. Currently, only X12 is supported. X12 is a set of standards and corresponding messages that define specific business documents.

transactionSet.description: Returns an enumerated type where each value identifies an X12 transaction set. Transaction sets are maintained by the X12 Accredited Standards Committee.

version.description: Returns the version to use for the specified X12 transaction set.

sampleDocument.description: Returns a sample EDI document that is used by a transformer as a guide for processing the EDI data.

inputConversion.description: Returns a structure that contains the format options for the transformation.

fromFormat.description: The format for the transformer input: currently on X12 is supported.

formatOptions.description: A structure that contains the formatting options for an inbound transformer.

x12.description:
A structure that contains the X12 transaction set and version. The X12 structure is used when the system transforms an EDI (electronic data interchange) file.

If an EDI input file contains more than one transaction, each transaction must have the same transaction set and version, for example 214/4010. If not, the transformer cannot parse the file.

transactionSet.description: Returns an enumerated type where each value identifies an X12 transaction set. Transaction sets are maintained by the X12 Accredited Standards Committee.

version.description: Returns the version to use for the specified X12 transaction set.

advancedOptions.description: Specifies advanced options for the input conversion process. These options provide additional control over how EDI files are processed during transformation.

x12.description: A structure that contains X12-specific advanced options, such as split options for processing X12 EDI files.

splitOptions.description: Specifies options for splitting X12 EDI files. These options control how large X12 files are divided into smaller, more manageable units.

validationOptions.description: Specifies validation options for X12 EDI processing. These options control how validation rules are applied during EDI document processing, including custom validation rules for element length constraints, code list validations, and element requirement checks.

mapping.description: Returns the structure that contains the mapping template and its language (either XSLT or JSONATA).

templateLanguage.description: The transformation language for the template, either XSLT or JSONATA.

template.description: A string that represents the mapping template, in the transformation language specified in templateLanguage.

outputConversion.description: Returns the OutputConversion object, which contains the format options for the outbound transformation.

toFormat.description: The format for the output from an outbound transformer: only X12 is currently supported.

formatOptions.description: A structure that contains the X12 transaction set and version for the transformer output.

x12.description:
A structure that contains the X12 transaction set and version. The X12 structure is used when the system transforms an EDI (electronic data interchange) file.

If an EDI input file contains more than one transaction, each transaction must have the same transaction set and version, for example 214/4010. If not, the transformer cannot parse the file.

transactionSet.description: Returns an enumerated type where each value identifies an X12 transaction set. Transaction sets are maintained by the X12 Accredited Standards Committee.

version.description: Returns the version to use for the specified X12 transaction set.

advancedOptions.description: A structure that contains advanced options for EDI processing. Currently, only X12 advanced options are supported.

x12.description: A structure that contains X12-specific advanced options, such as split options for processing X12 EDI files.

splitOptions.description: Specifies options for splitting X12 EDI files. These options control how large X12 files are divided into smaller, more manageable units.

validationOptions.description: Specifies validation options for X12 EDI processing. These options control how validation rules are applied during EDI document processing, including custom validation rules for element length constraints, code list validations, and element requirement checks.

sampleDocuments.description: Returns a structure that contains the Amazon S3 bucket and an array of the corresponding keys used to identify the location for your sample documents.

bucketName.description: Contains the Amazon S3 bucket that is used to hold your sample documents.

keys.description: Contains an array of the Amazon S3 keys used to identify the location for your sample documents.

capabilityId.description: Returns a system-assigned unique identifier for the capability.

capabilityArn.description: Returns an Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for a specific Amazon Web Services resource, such as a capability, partnership, profile, or transformer.

name.description: Returns the name of the capability used to identify it.

type.description: Returns the type of the capability. Currently, only edi is supported.

configuration.description: Returns a structure that contains the details for a capability.

instructionsDocuments.description: Returns one or more locations in Amazon S3, each specifying an EDI document that can be used with this capability. Each item contains the name of the bucket and the key, to identify the document's location.

createdAt.description: Returns a timestamp for creation date and time of the capability.

bucketName.description: Specifies the name of the Amazon S3 bucket.

key.description: Specifies the Amazon S3 key for the file location.

transactionSet.description: Returns an enumerated type where each value identifies an X12 transaction set. Transaction sets are maintained by the X12 Accredited Standards Committee.

version.description: Returns the version to use for the specified X12 transaction set.

status.description: Returns the current state of the transformer job, either running, succeeded, or failed.

outputFiles.description: Returns the location for the output files. If the caller specified a directory for the output, then this contains the full path to the output file, including the file name generated by the service.

message.description: Returns an optional error message, which gets populated when the job is not run successfully.

bucketName.description: Specifies the name of the Amazon S3 bucket.

key.description: Specifies the Amazon S3 key for the file location.

Tags.description: Returns the key-value pairs assigned to ARNs that you can use to group and search for resources by type. You can attach this metadata to resources (capabilities, partnerships, and so on) for any purpose.

Key.description: Specifies the name assigned to the tag that you create.

Value.description: Contains one or more values that you assigned to the key name that you create.

transformerJobId.description: Returns the unique, system-generated identifier for a transformer run.

validationMessages.description: Returns an array of validation messages that Amazon Web Services B2B Data Interchange generates during the conversion process. These messages include both standard EDI validation results and custom validation messages when custom validation rules are configured. Custom validation messages provide detailed feedback on element length constraints, code list validations, and element requirement checks applied during the outbound EDI generation process.

mappedFileContent.description: Returns a string for the mapping that can be used to identify the mapping. Similar to a fingerprint

parsedFileContent.description: Returns the contents of the input file being tested, parsed according to the specified EDI (electronic data interchange) type.

parsedSplitFileContents.description: Returns an array of parsed file contents when the input file is split according to the specified split options. Each element in the array represents a separate split file's parsed content.

validationMessages.description: Returns an array of validation messages generated during EDI validation. These messages provide detailed information about validation errors, warnings, or confirmations based on the configured X12 validation rules such as element length constraints, code list validations, and element requirement checks. This field is populated when the TestParsing API validates EDI documents.
